source_title: File Streaming Explained: Work on Large Files Without Downloading

concepts.name: File Streaming
concepts.definition: A data access model that allows users to access and work with files instantly from the cloud without downloading them in full, making them behave as if they were stored locally.

concepts.name: Sync and Share
concepts.definition: A traditional cloud storage model where files must be fully downloaded to a local device before they can be used and must be uploaded before changes are visible to others.

concepts.name: Sync Conflicts
concepts.definition: Issues that arise in sync-based workflows when multiple versions of the same file are created and modified simultaneously across different devices.

concepts.name: Source of Truth
concepts.definition: A centralized data location where the most current and authoritative version of a file resides, ensuring all collaborators see the same data in real time.

gotchas.description: Performance degradation and fragility with large files
gotchas.context: Occurs when using traditional 'Sync and Share' tools as file sizes increase (e.g., terabytes of data).
gotchas.solution: Implement File Streaming to stream only the required data rather than syncing the entire file.

gotchas.context: Occurs in traditional cloud storage when multiple users edit files and the system creates multiple conflicting copies.
gotchas.solution: Use a shared cloud file space (like Lucid Link) where everyone accesses the same source of truth directly from the cloud.
